metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1210
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig 2 and 4: SNAP-29-EGFP over expression inhibits repetitive (0.1 Hz) synaptic transmission and recovery after 20Hz vesicle pool depletion but has no effect on basal synaptic transmission in cultured hippocampal neurons measured in paired recordings of untransfected postsynaptic and SNAP-29 EGFP over expressing neurons.
Fig 7: shRNA-mediated KD of SNAP-29 results in increased postsynaptic currents
The authors conclude: These findings suggest that SNAP-29 acts as a negative modulator for neurotransmitter release, probably by slowing recycling of the SNARE-based fusion machinery and synaptic vesicle turnover.. Experimental description: Primary hippocampal neuron mixed cultures were prepared from embryonic Sprague-Dawley rat (age, embryonic day 17-18)

23/10/2017 Pim
- The overexpressed protein is of human species "Human SNAP-29 cDNA was amplified by PCR " Annotation to rat species is made based on origin of tissue and shRNA experiments.
